explicit_text: I was born in Hiroshima Ken, specifically in Asa Gun. My family were poor farmers there, and I grew up in a village where Buddhism, especially Nishihonganji, was the main religion. Although I was born in Hawaii, my roots and early memories are tied to Hiroshima Prefecture, where my parents lived and farmed.

explicit_text: I was actually born in Hawaii, though I was not registered there, so I became Japanese born. I lived in Hawaii until I was about 14 years old before returning to Japan for a few years. Later, I crossed the ocean back to Hawaii at age 19 and worked at a sugar cane company there for about two years before coming to the United States mainland.

explicit_text: When I was young in Japan, Christianity was viewed with suspicion and even hostility, seen as a religion of traitors. I was baptized in the Rikkyōkai church in San Francisco, and Christianity became a central part of my life.
